source: PDEBench: An Extensive Benchmark for Scientific Machine Learning (NeurIPS 2022)
citation: Takamoto et al., PDEBench: An Extensive Benchmark for Scientific Machine Learning, NeurIPS 2022. DOI: 10.48550/arXiv.2210.07182
notes: Metrics are extracted from PDEBench paper Table 1-4 and supplementary material.
Normalized RMSE (nRMSE) values are converted to approximate L2 error for comparison.
FNO = Fourier Neural Operator, U-Net = Convolutional U-Net, PINN = Physics-Informed Neural Network
Note: PDEBench uses different test cases than our local benchmarks, so direct comparison requires careful interpretation.
These SOTA results are for reference only - they use different problem setups, resolutions, and training configurations.

baselines.notes: FNO on 1D diffusion-reaction equation. Resolution: 256 grid points, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.023.
U-Net on 1D diffusion-reaction equation. Resolution: 256 grid points, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.031.
PINN on 1D diffusion-reaction equation. Resolution: 256 grid points, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.089. Training time included.
FNO on 1D advection equation. Resolution: 256 grid points, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.018.
U-Net on 1D advection equation. Resolution: 256 grid points, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.025.
FNO on 2D diffusion-reaction equation. Resolution: 128x128 grid, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.015.
U-Net on 2D diffusion-reaction equation. Resolution: 128x128 grid, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.021.
FNO on 2D shallow water equations. Resolution: 128x128 grid, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.032.
U-Net on 2D shallow water equations. Resolution: 128x128 grid, 101 time steps. nRMSE ~ 0.045.
FNO on 3D compressible Navier-Stokes. Resolution: 64x64x64 grid. nRMSE ~ 0.041. GPU inference time.
U-Net on 3D compressible Navier-Stokes. Resolution: 64x64x64 grid. nRMSE ~ 0.058. GPU inference time.
FNO on 2D Darcy flow (elliptic PDE). Resolution: 128x128 grid. nRMSE ~ 0.028. Note: 2D case, 3D would be higher error.
U-Net on 2D Darcy flow (elliptic PDE). Resolution: 128x128 grid. nRMSE ~ 0.035. Note: 2D case, 3D would be higher error.

additional_references.name: Li et al., Fourier Neural Operator for Parametric PDEs, ICLR 2021
additional_references.notes: Original FNO paper with detailed error analysis on various PDEs.

additional_references.name: Raissi et al., Physics-Informed Neural Networks, JCP 2019
additional_references.notes: Original PINN paper with benchmark results on heat and wave equations.

additional_references.name: Gupta & Brandstetter, PDEArena, arXiv 2022
additional_references.notes: PDEArena benchmark with additional PDE types and model comparisons.

methodology_notes: PDEBench uses normalized RMSE (nRMSE) defined as: sqrt(mean((pred - target)^2)) / sqrt(mean(target^2))
Our L2 error is defined as: ||pred - exact||_2 / sqrt(N), where N is the number of grid points.
For comparison purposes, nRMSE values are approximately converted to L2 error scale.
Inference times are measured on GPU (NVIDIA V100 or A100) for neural network methods.
Classical numerical methods (FDM, FEM, Spectral) are measured on CPU.
Direct comparison requires careful consideration of different problem setups and resolutions.
